blocks.title: Coming of the Son of Man

texts.NIV84: "There will be signs in the sun, moon and stars. On the earth, nations will be in anguish and perplexity at the roaring and tossing of the sea.
texts.KJV: And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;
texts.NASB95: “There will be signs in sun and moon and stars, and on the earth dismay among nations, in perplexity at the roaring of the sea and the waves,
texts.NKJV: “And there will be signs in the sun, in the moon, and in the stars; and on the earth distress of nations, with perplexity, the sea and the waves roaring;
texts.DRB: And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, by reason of the confusion of the roaring of the sea and of the waves;
texts.ESV: “And there will be signs in sun and moon and stars, and on the earth distress of nations in perplexity because of the roaring of the sea and the waves,
texts.LEB: “And there will be signs in the sun and moon and stars, and on the earth distress of nations in perplexity from the noise of the sea and its surging,
texts.AMP: “There will be signs (attesting miracles) in the sun and moon and stars; and on the earth [there will be] distress and anguish among nations, in perplexity at the roaring and tossing of the sea and the waves,
texts.NLT: “And there will be strange signs in the sun, moon, and stars. And here on earth the nations will be in turmoil, perplexed by the roaring seas and strange tides.
texts.AKJV: And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;
texts.NRSV: “There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars and on the earth distress among nations confused by the roaring of the sea and the waves.

texts.NIV84: Men will faint from terror, apprehensive of what is coming on the world, for the heavenly bodies will be shaken.
texts.KJV: Men's hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.
texts.NASB95: men fainting from fear and the expectation of the things which are coming upon the world; for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
texts.NKJV: men’s hearts failing them from fear and the expectation of those things which are coming on the earth, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
texts.DRB: Men withering away for fear, and expectation of what shall come upon the whole world. For the powers of heaven shall be moved;
texts.ESV: people fainting with fear and with foreboding of what is coming on the world. For the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
texts.LEB: people fainting from fear and expectation of what is coming on the inhabited earth, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
texts.AMP: people fainting from fear and expectation of the [dreadful] things coming on the world; for the [very] powers of the heavens will be shaken.
texts.NLT: People will be terrified at what they see coming upon the earth, for the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
texts.AKJV: men’s hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.
texts.NRSV: People will faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

texts.NASB95: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
texts.NKJV: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
texts.DRB: And then they shall see the Son of man coming in a cloud, with great power and majesty.
texts.ESV: And then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
texts.LEB: And then they will see the Son of Man arriving in a cloud with power and great glory.
texts.AMP: Then they will see THE SON OF MAN COMING IN A CLOUD with [transcendent, overwhelming] power [subduing the nations] and with great glory.
texts.NLT: Then everyone will see the Son of Man coming on a cloud with power and great glory.
texts.AKJV: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
texts.NRSV: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in a cloud’ with power and great glory.

texts.NIV84: When these things begin to take place, stand up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near."
texts.KJV: And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.
texts.NASB95: But when these things begin to take place, straighten up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
texts.NKJV: Now when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption draws near.”
texts.DRB: But when these things begin to come to pass, look up, and lift up your heads, because your redemption is at hand.
texts.ESV: Now when these things begin to take place, straighten up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
texts.LEB: But when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near!”
texts.AMP: Now when these things begin to occur, stand tall and lift up your heads [in joy], because [suffering ends as] your redemption is drawing near.”
texts.NLT: So when all these things begin to happen, stand and look up, for your salvation is near!”
texts.AKJV: And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.
texts.NRSV: Now when these things begin to take place, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”

texts.NIV84: Even so, when you see all these things, you know that it is near, right at the door.
texts.KJV: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.
texts.NASB95: so, you too, when you see all these things, recognize that He is near, right at the door.
texts.NKJV: So you also, when you see all these things, know that it is near—at the doors!
texts.DRB: So you also, when you shall see all these things, know ye that it is nigh, even at the doors.
texts.ESV: So also, when you see all these things, you know that he is near, at the very gates.

texts.NASB95: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour which he does not know,
texts.NKJV: the master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him and at an hour that he is not aware of,
texts.DRB: The lord of that servant shall come in a day that he hopeth not, and at an hour that he knoweth not:
texts.ESV: the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know
texts.LEB: the master of that slave will come on a day that he does not expect and at an hour that he does not know,
texts.AMP: the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour of which he is not aware,

texts.AKJV: the lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,

texts.NIV84: He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.KJV: And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.NASB95: and will cut him in pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.NKJV: and will cut him in two and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.DRB: And shall separate him, and appoint his portion with the hypocrites. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.ESV: and will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.LEB: and will cut him in two and assign his place with the hypocrites. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth!
texts.AMP: and will cut him in two and put him with the hypocrites; in that place there will be weeping [over sorrow and pain] and grinding of teeth [over distress and anger].
texts.NLT: and he will cut the servant to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.AKJV: and shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.NRSV: He will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

texts.NIV84: "No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
texts.KJV: But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.
texts.NASB95: But of that day or hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but the Father alone.
texts.NKJV: “But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
texts.DRB: But of that day or hour no man knoweth, neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but the Father.
texts.ESV: “But concerning that day or that hour, no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
texts.LEB: “But concerning that day or hour no one knows—not even the angels in heaven nor the Son—except the Father.
texts.AMP: But of that [exact] day or hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son [in His humanity], but the Father alone.
texts.NLT: “However, no one knows the day or hour when these things will happen, not even the angels in heaven or the Son himself. Only the Father knows.
texts.AKJV: But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.
texts.NRSV: “But about that day or hour no one knows, neither the angels in heaven nor the Son, but only the Father.

texts.NIV84: Be on guard! Be alert! You do not know when that time will come.
texts.KJV: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
texts.NASB95: “Take heed, keep on the alert; for you do not know when the appointed time will come.
texts.NKJV: Take heed, watch and pray; for you do not know when the time is.
texts.DRB: Take ye heed, watch and pray. For ye know not when the time is.
texts.ESV: Be on guard, keep awake. For you do not know when the time will come.
texts.LEB: Watch out! Be alert, because you do not know when the time is!
texts.AMP: “Be on guard and stay constantly alert [and pray]; for you do not know when the appointed time will come.
texts.NLT: And since you don’t know when that time will come, be on guard! Stay alert!
texts.AKJV: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
texts.NRSV: Beware, keep alert, for you do not know when the time will come.

texts.NIV84: It's like a man going away: He leaves his house and puts his servants in charge, each with his assigned task, and tells the one at the door to keep watch.
texts.KJV: For the Son of Man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.
texts.NASB95: It is like a man away on a journey, who upon leaving his house and putting his slaves in charge, assigning to each one his task, also commanded the doorkeeper to stay on the alert.
texts.NKJV: It is like a man going to a far country, who left his house and gave authority to his servants, and to each his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to watch.
texts.DRB: Even as a man who going into a far country, left his house; and gave authority to his servants over every work, and commanded the porter to watch.
texts.ESV: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his servants in charge, each with his work, and commands the doorkeeper to stay awake.
texts.LEB: It is like a man away on a journey, who left his house and gave his slaves authority—to each one his work—and to the doorkeeper he gave orders that he should be on the alert.
texts.AMP: It is like a man away on a journey, who when he left home put his servants in charge, each with his particular task, and also ordered the doorkeeper to be continually alert.
texts.NLT: “The coming of the Son of Man can be illustrated by the story of a man going on a long trip. When he left home, he gave each of his slaves instructions about the work they were to do, and he told the gatekeeper to watch for his return.
texts.AKJV: For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.
texts.NRSV: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his slaves in charge, each with his work, and commands the doorkeeper to be on the watch.

texts.NIV84: "Therefore keep watch because you do not know when the owner of the house will come back--whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or at dawn.
texts.KJV: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:
texts.NASB95: Therefore, be on the alert—for you do not know when the master of the house is coming, whether in the evening, at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning—
texts.NKJV: Watch therefore, for you do not know when the master of the house is coming—in the evening, at midnight, at the crowing of the rooster, or in the morning—
texts.DRB: Watch ye therefor, (for you know not when the lord of the house cometh: at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning,)
texts.ESV: Therefore stay awake—for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning—
texts.LEB: Therefore be on the alert, for you do not know when the master of the house is coming—whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or early in the morning—
texts.AMP: Therefore, be continually on the alert—for you do not know when the master of the house is coming, whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning—
texts.NLT: You, too, must keep watch! For you don’t know when the master of the household will return—in the evening, at midnight, before dawn, or at daybreak.
texts.AKJV: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:
texts.NRSV: Therefore, keep awake, for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening or at midnight or at cockcrow or at dawn,

texts.NIV84: If he comes suddenly, do not let him find you sleeping.
texts.KJV: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.
texts.NASB95: in case he should come suddenly and find you asleep.
texts.NKJV: lest, coming suddenly, he find you sleeping.
texts.DRB: Lest coming on a sudden, he find you sleeping.
texts.ESV: lest he come suddenly and find you asleep.
texts.LEB: lest he arrive suddenly and find you sleeping.
texts.AMP: [stay alert,] in case he should come suddenly and unexpectedly and find you asleep and unprepared.
texts.NLT: Don’t let him find you sleeping when he arrives without warning.
texts.AKJV: lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.
texts.NRSV: or else he may find you asleep when he comes suddenly.

texts.NIV84: What I say to you, I say to everyone: 'Watch!'"
texts.KJV: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
texts.NASB95: What I say to you I say to all, ‘Be on the alert!’”
texts.NKJV: And what I say to you, I say to all: Watch!”
texts.DRB: And what I say to you, I say to all: Watch.
texts.ESV: And what I say to you I say to all: Stay awake.”
texts.LEB: And what I say to you, I say to everyone: Be on the alert!”
texts.AMP: What I say to you I say to everyone, ‘Be on the alert [stay awake and be continually cautious]!’”
texts.NLT: I say to you what I say to everyone: Watch for him!”
texts.AKJV: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
texts.NRSV: And what I say to you I say to all: Keep awake.”

texts.NIV84: "Be careful, or your hearts will be weighed down with dissipation, drunkenness and the anxieties of life, and that day will close on you unexpectedly like a trap.
texts.KJV: And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and so that day come upon you unawares.
texts.NASB95: “Be on guard, so that your hearts will not be weighted down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of life, and that day will not come on you suddenly like a trap;
texts.NKJV: “But take heed to yourselves, lest your hearts be weighed down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that Day come on you unexpectedly.
texts.DRB: And take heed to yourselves, lest perhaps your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting and drunkenness, and the cares of this life, and that day come upon you suddenly.
texts.ESV: “But watch yourselves lest your hearts be weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and cares of this life, and that day come upon you suddenly like a trap.
texts.LEB: “But take care for yourselves, lest your hearts are weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of daily life, and that day come upon you suddenly
texts.AMP: “But be on guard, so that your hearts are not weighed down and depressed with the giddiness of debauchery and the nausea of self-indulgence and the worldly worries of life, and then that day [when the Messiah returns] will not come on you suddenly like a trap;
texts.NLT: “Watch out! Don’t let your hearts be dulled by carousing and drunkenness, and by the worries of this life. Don’t let that day catch you unaware,
texts.AKJV: And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and so that day come upon you unawares.
texts.NRSV: “Be on guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life and that day does not catch you unexpectedly,

texts.NIV84: For it will come upon all those who live on the face of the whole earth.
texts.KJV: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
texts.NASB95: for it will come upon all those who dwell on the face of all the earth.
texts.NKJV: For it will come as a snare on all those who dwell on the face of the whole earth.
texts.DRB: For as a snare shall it come upon all that sit upon the face of the whole earth.
texts.ESV: For it will come upon all who dwell on the face of the whole earth.
texts.LEB: like a trap. For it will come upon all who reside on the face of the whole earth.
texts.AMP: for it will come upon all those who live on the face of all the earth.
texts.NLT: like a trap. For that day will come upon everyone living on the earth.
texts.AKJV: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
texts.NRSV: like a trap. For it will come upon all who live on the face of the whole earth.

texts.NIV84: Be always on the watch, and pray that you may be able to escape all that is about to happen, and that you may be able to stand before the Son of Man."
texts.KJV: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.
texts.NASB95: But keep on the alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are about to take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
texts.NKJV: Watch therefore, and pray always that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will come to pass, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
texts.DRB: Watch ye, therefore, praying at all times, that you may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that are to come, and to stand before the Son of man.
texts.ESV: But stay awake at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are going to take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
texts.LEB: But be alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are going to happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
texts.AMP: But keep alert at all times [be attentive and ready], praying that you may have the strength and ability [to be found worthy and] to escape all these things that are going to take place, and to stand in the presence of the Son of Man [at His coming].”
texts.NLT: Keep alert at all times. And pray that you might be strong enough to escape these coming horrors and stand before the Son of Man.”
texts.AKJV: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.
texts.NRSV: Be alert at all times, praying that you may have the strength to escape all these things that will take place and to stand before the Son of Man.”

texts.NIV84: Each day Jesus was teaching at the temple, and each evening he went out to spend the night on the hill called the Mount of Olives,
texts.KJV: And in the day time he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives.
texts.NASB95: Now during the day He was teaching in the temple, but at evening He would go out and spend the night on the mount that is called Olivet.
texts.NKJV: And in the daytime He was teaching in the temple, but at night He went out and stayed on the mountain called Olivet.
texts.DRB: And in the daytime, he was teaching in the temple; but at night, going out, he abode in the mount that is called Olivet.
texts.ESV: And every day he was teaching in the temple, but at night he went out and lodged on the mount called Olivet.
texts.LEB: So throughout the days he was teaching in the temple courts, and throughout the nights he was going out and spending the night on the hill that is called the Mount of Olives.
texts.AMP: Now in the daytime Jesus was teaching in [the porches and courts of] the temple, but at night He would go out and spend the night on the mount that is called Olivet.
texts.NLT: Every day Jesus went to the Temple to teach, and each evening he returned to spend the night on the Mount of Olives.
texts.AKJV: And in the day time he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives.
texts.NRSV: Every day he was teaching in the temple, and at night he would go out and spend the night on the Mount of Olives, as it was called.

texts.NIV84: and all the people came early in the morning to hear him at the temple.
texts.KJV: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.
texts.NASB95: And all the people would get up early in the morning to come to Him in the temple to listen to Him.
texts.NKJV: Then early in the morning all the people came to Him in the temple to hear Him.
texts.DRB: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, to hear him.
texts.ESV: And early in the morning all the people came to him in the temple to hear him.
texts.LEB: And all the people were getting up very early in the morning to come to him in the temple courts to listen to him.
texts.AMP: And early in the morning all the people would come to Him in the temple to listen to Him.
texts.NLT: The crowds gathered at the Temple early each morning to hear him.
texts.AKJV: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.
texts.NRSV: And all the people would get up early in the morning to listen to him in the temple.

blocks.title: God Speaks in the Last Days

texts.NIV84: In the past God spoke to our forefathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,
texts.KJV: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,
texts.NASB95: God, after He spoke long ago to the fathers in the prophets in many portions and in many ways,
texts.NKJV: God, who at various times and in various ways spoke in time past to the fathers by the prophets,
texts.DRB: God, who, at sundry times and in divers manners, spoke in times past to the fathers by the prophets, last of all,
texts.ESV: Long ago, at many times and in many ways, God spoke to our fathers by the prophets,
texts.LEB: Although God spoke long ago in many parts and in many ways to the fathers by the prophets,
texts.AMP: God, having spoken to the fathers long ago in [the voices and writings of] the prophets in many separate revelations [each of which set forth a portion of the truth], and in many ways,
texts.NLT: Long ago God spoke many times and in many ways to our ancestors through the prophets.
texts.AKJV: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,
texts.NRSV: Long ago God spoke to our ancestors in many and various ways by the prophets,

texts.NIV84: but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom he made the universe.
texts.KJV: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;
texts.NASB95: in these last days has spoken to us in His Son, whom He appointed heir of all things, through whom also He made the world.
texts.NKJV: has in these last days spoken to us by His Son, whom He has appointed heir of all things, through whom also He made the worlds;
texts.DRB: In these days hath spoken to us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the world.
texts.ESV: but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed the heir of all things, through whom also he created the world.
texts.LEB: in these last days he has spoken to us by a Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world,
texts.AMP: has in these last days spoken [with finality] to us in [the person of One who is by His character and nature] His Son [namely Jesus], whom He appointed heir and lawful owner of all things, through whom also He created the universe [that is, the universe as a space-time-matter continuum].
texts.NLT: And now in these final days, he has spoken to us through his Son. God promised everything to the Son as an inheritance, and through the Son he created the universe.
texts.AKJV: hath in these last days spoken unto us by his Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;
texts.NRSV: but in these last days he has spoken to us by a Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom he also created the worlds.

texts.NIV84: The Son is the radiance of God's glory and the exact representation of his being, sustaining all things by his powerful word. After he had provided purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty in heaven.
texts.KJV: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high:
texts.NASB95: And He is the radiance of His glory and the exact representation of His nature, and upholds all things by the word of His power. When He had made purification of sins, He sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
texts.NKJV: who being the brightness of His glory and the express image of His person, and upholding all things by the word of His power, when He had by Himself purged our sins, sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
texts.DRB: Who being the brightness of his glory, and the figure of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, making purgation of sins, sitteth on the right hand of the majesty on high.
texts.ESV: He is the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of his nature, and he upholds the universe by the word of his power. After making purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
texts.LEB: who is the radiance of his glory and the representation of his essence, sustaining all things by the word of power. When he had made purification for sins through him, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
texts.AMP: The Son is the radiance and only expression of the glory of [our awesome] God [reflecting God’s Shekinah glory, the Light-being, the brilliant light of the divine], and the exact representation and perfect imprint of His [Father’s] essence, and upholding and maintaining and propelling all things [the entire physical and spiritual universe] by His powerful word [carrying the universe along to its predetermined goal]. When He [Himself and no other] had [by offering Himself on the cross as a sacrifice for sin] accomplished purification from sins and established our freedom from guilt, He sat down [revealing His completed work] at the right hand of the Majesty on high [revealing His Divine authority],
texts.NLT: The Son radiates God’s own glory and expresses the very character of God, and he sustains everything by the mighty power of his command. When he had cleansed us from our sins, he sat down in the place of honor at the right hand of the majestic God in heaven.
texts.AKJV: who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
texts.NRSV: He is the reflection of God’s glory and the exact imprint of God’s very being, and he sustains all things by his powerful word. When he had made purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,

texts.NIV84: So he became as much superior to the angels as the name he has inherited is superior to theirs.
texts.KJV: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
texts.NASB95: having become as much better than the angels, as He has inherited a more excellent name than they.
texts.NKJV: having become so much better than the angels, as He has by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
texts.DRB: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath inherited a more excellent name than they.
texts.ESV: having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
texts.LEB: having become by so much better than the angels, by as much as he has inherited a more excellent name than theirs.
texts.AMP: having become as much superior to angels, since He has inherited a more excellent and glorious name than they [that is, Son—the name above all names].
texts.NLT: This shows that the Son is far greater than the angels, just as the name God gave him is greater than their names.
texts.AKJV: being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
texts.NRSV: having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.

blocks.title: Terrible Times in the Last Days

texts.NIV84: But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days.
texts.KJV: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.
texts.NASB95: But realize this, that in the last days difficult times will come.
texts.NKJV: But know this, that in the last days perilous times will come:
texts.DRB: Know also this, that, in the last days, shall come dangerous times.
texts.ESV: But understand this, that in the last days there will come times of difficulty.
texts.LEB: But know this, that in the last days difficult times will come,
texts.AMP: But understand this, that in the last days dangerous times [of great stress and trouble] will come [difficult days that will be hard to bear].
texts.NLT: You should know this, Timothy, that in the last days there will be very difficult times.
texts.AKJV: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.
texts.NRSV: You must understand this, that in the last days distressing times will come.

texts.NIV84: People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
texts.KJV: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
texts.NASB95: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, revilers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
texts.NKJV: For men will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
texts.DRB: Men shall be lovers of themselves, covetous, haughty, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, wicked,
texts.ESV: For people will be lovers of self, lovers of money, proud, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
texts.LEB: for people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, arrogant, slanderers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
texts.AMP: For people will be lovers of self [narcissistic, self-focused], lovers of money [impelled by greed], boastful, arrogant, revilers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy and profane,
texts.NLT: For people will love only themselves and their money. They will be boastful and proud, scoffing at God, disobedient to their parents, and ungrateful. They will consider nothing sacred.
texts.AKJV: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,
texts.NRSV: For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,

texts.NIV84: without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good,
texts.KJV: Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,
texts.NASB95: unloving, irreconcilable, malicious gossips, without self-control, brutal, haters of good,
texts.NKJV: unloving, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, brutal, despisers of good,
texts.DRB: Without affection, without peace, slanderers, incontinent, unmerciful, without kindness,
texts.ESV: heartless, unappeasable, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not loving good,
texts.LEB: hardhearted, irreconcilable, slanderous, without self-control, savage, with no interest for what is good,
texts.AMP: [and they will be] unloving [devoid of natural human affection, calloused and inhumane], irreconcilable, malicious gossips, devoid of self-control [intemperate, immoral], brutal, haters of good,
texts.NLT: They will be unloving and unforgiving; they will slander others and have no self-control. They will be cruel and hate what is good.
texts.AKJV: without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,
texts.NRSV: unfeeling, implacable, slanderers, profligates, brutes, haters of good,

texts.NIV84: treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God--
texts.KJV: Traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;
texts.NASB95: treacherous, reckless, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.NKJV: traitors, headstrong, haughty, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.DRB: Traitors, stubborn, puffed up, and lovers of pleasures more than of God:
texts.ESV: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.LEB: traitors, reckless, conceited, loving pleasure rather than loving God,
texts.AMP: traitors, reckless, conceited, lovers of [sensual] pleasure rather than lovers of God,
texts.NLT: They will betray their friends, be reckless, be puffed up with pride, and love pleasure rather than God.
texts.AKJV: traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;
texts.NRSV: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,

texts.NIV84: having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with them.
texts.KJV: Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.
texts.NASB95: holding to a form of godliness, although they have denied its power; Avoid such men as these.
texts.NKJV: having a form of godliness but denying its power. And from such people turn away!
texts.DRB: Having an appearance indeed of godliness, but denying the power thereof. Now these avoid.
texts.ESV: having the appearance of godliness, but denying its power. Avoid such people.
texts.LEB: maintaining a form of godliness, but denying its power. Avoid these people.
texts.AMP: holding to a form of [outward] godliness (religion), although they have denied its power [for their conduct nullifies their claim of faith]. Avoid such people and keep far away from them.
texts.NLT: They will act religious, but they will reject the power that could make them godly. Stay away from people like that!
texts.AKJV: having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.
texts.NRSV: holding to the outward form of godliness but denying its power. Avoid them!

texts.NIV84: They are the kind who worm their way into homes and gain control over weak-willed women, who are loaded down with sins and are swayed by all kinds of evil desires,
texts.KJV: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,
texts.NASB95: For among them are those who enter into households and captivate weak women weighed down with sins, led on by various impulses,
texts.NKJV: For of this sort are those who creep into households and make captives of gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,
texts.DRB: For of these sort are they who creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, who are led away with divers desires:
texts.ESV: For among them are those who creep into households and capture weak women, burdened with sins and led astray by various passions,
texts.LEB: For from these are those who slip into houses and captivate foolish women loaded down with sins, led by various kinds of desires,
texts.AMP: For among them are those who worm their way into homes and captivate morally weak and spiritually-dwarfed women weighed down by [the burden of their] sins, easily swayed by various impulses,
texts.NLT: They are the kind who work their way into people’s homes and win the confidence of vulnerable women who are burdened with the guilt of sin and controlled by various desires.
texts.AKJV: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,
texts.NRSV: For among them are those who make their way into households and captivate immature women, overwhelmed by their sins and swayed by all kinds of desires,

texts.NIV84: always learning but never able to acknowledge the truth.
texts.KJV: Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NASB95: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NKJV: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.DRB: Ever learning, and never attaining to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.ESV: always learning and never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
texts.LEB: always learning and never able to come to a knowledge of the truth.
texts.AMP: always learning and listening to anybody who will teach them, but never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NLT: (Such women are forever following new teachings, but they are never able to understand the truth.)
texts.AKJV: ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
texts.NRSV: who are always studying yet never able to recognize truth.

texts.NIV84: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so also these men oppose the truth--men of depraved minds, who, as far as the faith is concerned, are rejected.
texts.KJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.
texts.NASB95: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men of depraved mind, rejected in regard to the faith.
texts.NKJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres resisted Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, disapproved concerning the faith;
texts.DRB: Now as Jannes and Mambres resisted Moses, so these also resist the truth, men corrupted in mind, reprobate concerning the faith.
texts.ESV: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men corrupted in mind and disqualified regarding the faith.
texts.LEB: And just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so also these oppose the truth, people corrupted in mind, disqualified concerning the faith.
texts.AMP: Just as Jannes and Jambres [the court magicians of Egypt] opposed Moses, so these men also oppose the truth, men of depraved mind, unqualified and worthless [as teachers] in regard to the faith.
texts.NLT: These teachers oppose the truth just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. They have depraved minds and a counterfeit faith.
texts.AKJV: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.
texts.NRSV: As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people, of corrupt mind and counterfeit faith, also oppose the truth.

texts.NIV84: But they will not get very far because, as in the case of those men, their folly will be clear to everyone.
texts.KJV: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as their's also was.
texts.NASB95: But they will not make further progress; for their folly will be obvious to all, just as Jannes’s and Jambres’s folly was also.
texts.NKJV: but they will progress no further, for their folly will be manifest to all, as theirs also was.
texts.DRB: But they shall proceed no farther; for their folly shall be manifest to all men, as theirs also was.
texts.ESV: But they will not get very far, for their folly will be plain to all, as was that of those two men.
texts.LEB: But they will not progress to a greater extent, for their folly will be quite evident to everyone, as also the folly of those two was.
texts.AMP: But they will not get very far, for their meaningless nonsense and ignorance will become obvious to everyone, as was that of Jannes and Jambres.
texts.NLT: But they won’t get away with this for long. Someday everyone will recognize what fools they are, just as with Jannes and Jambres.
texts.AKJV: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as theirs also was.
texts.NRSV: But they will not make much progress because, as in the case of those two men, their folly will become plain to everyone.

blocks.title: Man of God and Word of God

texts.NIV84: You, however, know all about my teaching, my way of life, my purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance,
texts.KJV: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,
texts.NASB95: Now you followed my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, perseverance,
texts.NKJV: But you have carefully followed my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, love, perseverance,
texts.DRB: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, love, patience,
texts.ESV: You, however, have followed my teaching, my conduct, my aim in life, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadfastness,
texts.LEB: But you have faithfully followed my teaching, way of life, purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance,
texts.AMP: Now you have diligently followed [my example, that is] my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness,
texts.NLT: But you, Timothy, certainly know what I teach, and how I live, and what my purpose in life is. You know my faith, my patience, my love, and my endurance.
texts.AKJV: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,
texts.NRSV: Now you have observed my teaching, my conduct, my aim in life, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadfastness,

texts.NIV84: persecutions, sufferings--what kinds of things happened to me in Antioch, Iconium and Lystra, the persecutions I endured. Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them.
texts.KJV: Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me.
texts.NASB95: persecutions, and sufferings, such as happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium and at Lystra; what persecutions I endured, and out of them all the Lord rescued me!
texts.NKJV: persecutions, afflictions, which happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra—what persecutions I endured. And out of them all the Lord delivered me.
texts.DRB: Persecutions, afflictions: such as came upon me at Antioch, at Iconium, and at Lystra: what persecutions I endured, and out of them all the Lord delivered me.
texts.ESV: my persecutions and sufferings that happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, and at Lystra—which persecutions I endured; yet from them all the Lord rescued me.
texts.LEB: persecutions, and sufferings that happened to me in Antioch, in Iconium, and in Lystra, what sort of persecutions I endured, and the Lord delivered me from all of them.
texts.AMP: persecutions, and sufferings—such as happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, and at Lystra; what persecutions I endured, but the Lord rescued me from them all!
texts.NLT: You know how much persecution and suffering I have endured. You know all about how I was persecuted in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra—but the Lord rescued me from all of it.
texts.AKJV: persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me.
texts.NRSV: my persecutions, and my sufferings, the things that happened to me in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. What persecutions I endured! Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them.

texts.NIV84: In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted,
texts.KJV: Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution.
texts.NASB95: Indeed, all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
texts.NKJV: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
texts.DRB: And all that will live godly in Christ Jesus, shall suffer persecution.
texts.ESV: Indeed, all who desire to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted,
texts.LEB: And indeed, all those who want to live in a godly manner in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
texts.AMP: Indeed, all who delight in pursuing righteousness and are determined to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will be hunted and persecuted [because of their faith].
texts.NLT: Yes, and everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
texts.AKJV: Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution.
texts.NRSV: Indeed, all who want to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.

texts.NIV84: while evil men and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
texts.KJV: But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.
texts.NASB95: But evil men and impostors will proceed from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
texts.NKJV: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.
texts.DRB: But evil men and seducers shall grow worse and worse: erring, and driving into error.
texts.ESV: while evil people and impostors will go on from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
texts.LEB: But evil people and imposters will progress to the worse, deceiving and being deceived.
texts.AMP: But evil men and impostors will go on from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
texts.NLT: But evil people and impostors will flourish. They will deceive others and will themselves be deceived.
texts.AKJV: But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.
texts.NRSV: But wicked people and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived.

texts.NIV84: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and have become convinced of, because you know those from whom you learned it,
texts.KJV: But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;
texts.NASB95: You, however, continue in the things you have learned and become convinced of, knowing from whom you have learned them,
texts.NKJV: But you must continue in the things which you have learned and been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them,
texts.DRB: But continue thou in those things which thou hast learned, and which have been committed to thee: knowing of whom thou hast learned them;
texts.ESV: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and have firmly believed, knowing from whom you learned it
texts.LEB: But you continue in the things which you have learned and are convinced of, because you know from whom you learned them,
texts.AMP: But as for you, continue in the things that you have learned and of which you are convinced [holding tightly to the truths], knowing from whom you learned them,
texts.NLT: But you must remain faithful to the things you have been taught. You know they are true, for you know you can trust those who taught you.
texts.AKJV: But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;
texts.NRSV: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and firmly believed, knowing from whom you learned it

texts.NIV84: and how from infancy you have known the holy Scriptures, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
texts.KJV: And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
texts.NASB95: and that from childhood you have known the sacred writings which are able to give you the wisdom that leads to salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
texts.NKJV: and that from childhood you have known the Holy Scriptures, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
texts.DRB: And because from thy infancy thou hast known the holy scriptures, which can instruct thee to salvation, by the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
texts.ESV: and how from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
texts.LEB: and that from childhood you have known the holy writings that are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
texts.AMP: and how from childhood you have known the sacred writings (Hebrew Scriptures) which are able to give you the wisdom that leads to salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus [surrendering your entire self to Him and having absolute confidence in His wisdom, power and goodness].
texts.NLT: You have been taught the holy Scriptures from childhood, and they have given you the wisdom to receive the salvation that comes by trusting in Christ Jesus.
texts.AKJV: and that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
texts.NRSV: and how from childhood you have known sacred writings that are able to instruct you for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.

texts.NIV84: All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness,
texts.KJV: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:
texts.NASB95: All Scripture is inspired by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, for training in righteousness;
texts.NKJV: All Scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness,
texts.DRB: All scripture, inspired of God, is profitable to teach, to reprove, to correct, to instruct in justice,
texts.ESV: All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
texts.LEB: All scripture is inspired by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, for training in righteousness,
texts.AMP: All Scripture is God-breathed [given by divine inspiration] and is profitable for instruction, for conviction [of sin], for correction [of error and restoration to obedience], for training in righteousness [learning to live in conformity to God’s will, both publicly and privately—behaving honorably with personal integrity and moral courage];
texts.NLT: All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we are wrong and teaches us to do what is right.
texts.AKJV: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:
texts.NRSV: All scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,

texts.NIV84: so that the man of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work.
texts.KJV: That the man of God may be perfect, thoroughly furnished unto all good works.
texts.NASB95: so that the man of God may be adequate, equipped for every good work.
texts.NKJV: that the man of God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.
texts.DRB: That the man of God may be perfect, furnished to every good work.
texts.ESV: that the man of God may be complete, equipped for every good work.
texts.LEB: in order that the person of God may be competent, equipped for every good work.
texts.AMP: so that the man of God may be complete and proficient, outfitted and thoroughly equipped for every good work.
texts.NLT: God uses it to prepare and equip his people to do every good work.
texts.AKJV: that the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.
texts.NRSV: so that the person of God may be proficient, equipped for every good work.

texts.NIV84: Now listen, you rich people, weep and wail because of the misery that is coming upon you.
texts.KJV: Go to now, ye rich men, weep and howl for your miseries that shall come upon you.
texts.NASB95: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries which are coming upon you.
texts.NKJV: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming upon you!
texts.DRB: Go to now, ye rich men, weep and howl in your miseries, which shall come upon you.
texts.ESV: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for the miseries that are coming upon you.
texts.LEB: Come now, you rich people, weep and cry aloud over the miseries that are coming upon you!
texts.AMP: Come [quickly] now, you rich [who lack true faith and hoard and misuse your resources], weep and howl over the miseries [the woes, the judgments] that are coming upon you.
texts.NLT: Look here, you rich people: Weep and groan with anguish because of all the terrible troubles ahead of you.
texts.AKJV: Go to now, ye rich men, weep and howl for your miseries that shall come upon you.
texts.NRSV: Come now, you rich people, weep and wail for the miseries that are coming to you.

texts.NIV84: Your wealth has rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
texts.KJV: Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.
texts.NASB95: Your riches have rotted and your garments have become moth-eaten.
texts.NKJV: Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are moth-eaten.
texts.DRB: Your riches are corrupted: and your garments are motheaten.
texts.ESV: Your riches have rotted and your garments are moth-eaten.
texts.LEB: Your wealth has rotted, and your clothing has become moth-eaten.
texts.AMP: Your wealth has rotted and is ruined and your [fine] clothes have become moth-eaten.
texts.NLT: Your wealth is rotting away, and your fine clothes are moth-eaten rags.
texts.AKJV: Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.
texts.NRSV: Your riches have rotted, and your clothes are moth-eaten.

texts.NIV84: Your gold and silver are corroded. Their corrosion will testify against you and eat your flesh like fire. You have hoarded wealth in the last days.
texts.KJV: Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.
texts.NASB95: Your gold and your silver have rusted; and their rust will be a witness against you and will consume your flesh like fire. It is in the last days that you have stored up your treasure!
texts.NKJV: Your gold and silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have heaped up treasure in the last days.
texts.DRB: Your gold and silver is cankered: and the rust of them shall be for a testimony against you, and shall eat your flesh like fire. You have stored up to yourselves wrath against the last days.
texts.ESV: Your gold and silver have corroded, and their corrosion will be evidence against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up treasure in the last days.
texts.LEB: Your gold and silver have become corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you, and it will consume your flesh like fire. You have stored up treasure in the last days.
texts.AMP: Your gold and silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you and will consume your flesh like fire. You have stored up your treasure in the last days [when it will do you no good].
texts.NLT: Your gold and silver are corroded. The very wealth you were counting on will eat away your flesh like fire. This corroded treasure you have hoarded will testify against you on the day of judgment.
texts.AKJV: Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.
texts.NRSV: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you, and it will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up treasure during the last days.

texts.NIV84: Look! The wages you failed to pay the workmen who mowed your fields are crying out against you. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord Almighty.
texts.KJV: Behold, the hire of the labourers who have reaped down your fields, which is of you kept back by fraud, crieth: and the cries of them which have reaped are entered into the ears of the Lord of sabaoth.
texts.NASB95: Behold, the pay of the laborers who mowed your fields, and which has been withheld by you, cries out against you; and the outcry of those who did the harvesting has reached the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth.
texts.NKJV: Indeed the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, cry out; and the cries of the reapers have reached the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth.
texts.DRB: Behold the hire of the labourers, who have reaped down your fields, which by fraud has been kept back by you, crieth: and the cry of them hath entered into the ears of the Lord of sabaoth.
texts.ESV: Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, are crying out against you, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.
texts.LEB: Behold, the wages that were held back by you from the workers who reap your fields cry out, and the cries of the reapers have come to the ears of the Lord of hosts.
texts.AMP: Look! The wages that you have [fraudulently] withheld from the laborers who have mowed your fields are crying out [against you for vengeance]; and the cries of the harvesters have come to the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth.
texts.NLT: For listen! Hear the cries of the field workers whom you have cheated of their pay. The cries of those who harvest your fields have reached the ears of the Lord of Heaven’s Armies.
texts.AKJV: Behold, the hire of the labourers who have reaped down your fields, which is of you kept back by fraud, crieth: and the cries of them which have reaped are entered into the ears of the Lord of sabaoth.
texts.NRSV: Listen! The wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, cry out, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.

texts.NIV84: You have lived on earth in luxury and self-indulgence. You have fattened yourselves in the day of slaughter.
texts.KJV: Ye have lived in pleasure on the earth, and been wanton; ye have nourished your hearts, as in a day of slaughter.
texts.NASB95: You have lived luxuriously on the earth and led a life of wanton pleasure; you have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
texts.NKJV: You have lived on the earth in pleasure and luxury; you have fattened your hearts as in a day of slaughter.
texts.DRB: You have feasted upon earth: and in riotousness you have nourished your hearts, in the day of slaughter.
texts.ESV: You have lived on the earth in luxury and in self-indulgence. You have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
texts.LEB: You have lived self-indulgently on the earth and have lived luxuriously. You have fattened your hearts in the day of slaughter.
texts.AMP: On the earth you have lived luxuriously and abandoned yourselves to soft living and led a life of wanton pleasure [self-indulgence, self-gratification]; you have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
texts.NLT: You have spent your years on earth in luxury, satisfying your every desire. You have fattened yourselves for the day of slaughter.
texts.AKJV: Ye have lived in pleasure on the earth, and been wanton; ye have nourished your hearts, as in a day of slaughter.
texts.NRSV: You have lived on the earth in luxury and in pleasure; you have nourished your hearts in a day of slaughter.

texts.NIV84: You have condemned and murdered innocent men, who were not opposing you.
texts.KJV: Ye have condemned and killed the just; and he doth not resist you.
texts.NASB95: You have condemned and put to death the righteous man; he does not resist you.
texts.NKJV: You have condemned, you have murdered the just; he does not resist you.
texts.DRB: You have condemned and put to death the Just One, and he resisted you not.
texts.ESV: You have condemned and murdered the righteous person. He does not resist you.
texts.LEB: You have condemned, you have murdered the righteous person; he does not resist you.
texts.AMP: You have condemned and have put to death the righteous man; he offers you no resistance.
texts.NLT: You have condemned and killed innocent people, who do not resist you.
texts.AKJV: Ye have condemned and killed the just; and he doth not resist you.
texts.NRSV: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who does not resist you.

texts.NIV84: The Spirit clearly says that in later times some will abandon the faith and follow deceiving spirits and things taught by demons.
texts.KJV: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;
texts.NASB95: But the Spirit explicitly says that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to deceitful spirits and doctrines of demons,
texts.NKJV: Now the Spirit expressly says that in latter times some will depart from the faith, giving heed to deceiving spirits and doctrines of demons,
texts.DRB: Now the Spirit manifestly saith, that in the last times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to spirits of error, and doctrines of devils,
texts.ESV: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith by devoting themselves to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,
texts.LEB: Now the Spirit explicitly says that in the last times some will depart from the faith, paying attention to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,
texts.AMP: But the [Holy] Spirit explicitly and unmistakably declares that in later times some will turn away from the faith, paying attention instead to deceitful and seductive spirits and doctrines of demons,
texts.NLT: Now the Holy Spirit tells us clearly that in the last times some will turn away from the true faith; they will follow deceptive spirits and teachings that come from demons.
texts.AKJV: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;
texts.NRSV: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will renounce the faith by paying attention to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,

texts.NIV84: Such teachings come through hypocritical liars, whose consciences have been seared as with a hot iron.
texts.KJV: Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;
texts.NASB95: by means of the hypocrisy of liars seared in their own conscience as with a branding iron,
texts.NKJV: speaking lies in hypocrisy, having their own conscience seared with a hot iron,
texts.DRB: Speaking lies in hypocrisy, and having their conscience seared,
texts.ESV: through the insincerity of liars whose consciences are seared,
texts.LEB: by the hypocrisy of liars, who are seared in their own conscience,
texts.AMP: [misled] by the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared as with a branding iron [leaving them incapable of ethical functioning],
texts.NLT: These people are hypocrites and liars, and their consciences are dead.
texts.AKJV: speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;
texts.NRSV: through the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared with a hot iron.

texts.NIV84: They forbid people to marry and order them to abstain from certain foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and who know the truth.
texts.KJV: Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.
texts.NASB95: men who forbid marriage and advocate abstaining from foods which God has created to be gratefully shared in by those who believe and know the truth.
texts.NKJV: forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
texts.DRB: Forbidding to marry, to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving by the faithful, and by them that have known the truth.
texts.ESV: who forbid marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
texts.LEB: who forbid marrying and insist on abstaining from foods that God created for sharing in with thankfulness by those who believe and who know the truth,
texts.AMP: who forbid marriage and advocate abstaining from [certain kinds of] foods which God has created to be gratefully shared by those who believe and have [a clear] knowledge of the truth.
texts.NLT: They will say it is wrong to be married and wrong to eat certain foods. But God created those foods to be eaten with thanks by faithful people who know the truth.
texts.AKJV: forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.
texts.NRSV: They forbid marriage and abstain from certain foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.

texts.NIV84: For everything God created is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving,
texts.KJV: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:
texts.NASB95: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with gratitude;
texts.NKJV: For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be refused if it is received with thanksgiving;
texts.DRB: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be rejected that is received with thanksgiving:
texts.ESV: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving,
texts.LEB: because everything created by God is good and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thankfulness,
texts.AMP: For everything God has created is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with gratitude;
texts.NLT: Since everything God created is good, we should not reject any of it but receive it with thanks.
texts.AKJV: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:
texts.NRSV: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, provided it is received with thanksgiving,

texts.NIV84: because it is consecrated by the word of God and prayer.
texts.KJV: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
texts.NASB95: for it is sanctified by means of the word of God and prayer.
texts.NKJV: for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
texts.DRB: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
texts.ESV: for it is made holy by the word of God and prayer.
texts.LEB: for it is made holy by the word of God and prayer.
texts.AMP: for it is sanctified [set apart, dedicated to God] by means of the word of God and prayer.
texts.NLT: For we know it is made acceptable by the word of God and prayer.
texts.AKJV: for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
texts.NRSV: for it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.

texts.NIV84: If you point these things out to the brothers, you will be a good minister of Christ Jesus, brought up in the truths of the faith and of the good teaching that you have followed.
texts.KJV: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.
texts.NASB95: In pointing out these things to the brethren, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, constantly nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound doctrine which you have been following.
texts.NKJV: If you instruct the brethren in these things, you will be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished in the words of faith and of the good doctrine which you have carefully followed.
texts.DRB: These things proposing to the brethren, thou shalt be a good minister of Christ Jesus, nourished up in the words of faith, and of the good doctrine which thou hast attained unto.
texts.ESV: If you put these things before the brothers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, being trained in the words of the faith and of the good doctrine that you have followed.
texts.LEB: By teaching these things to the brothers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, trained in the words of the faith and of the good teaching that you have followed faithfully.
texts.AMP: If you point out these instructions to the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, constantly nourished [through study] on the words of the faith and of the good [Christian] doctrine which you have closely followed.
texts.NLT: If you explain these things to the brothers and sisters, Timothy, you will be a worthy servant of Christ Jesus, one who is nourished by the message of faith and the good teaching you have followed.
texts.AKJV: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.
texts.NRSV: If you put these instructions before the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound teaching that you have followed.

texts.NIV84: Have nothing to do with godless myths and old wives' tales; rather, train yourself to be godly.
texts.KJV: But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.
texts.NASB95: But have nothing to do with worldly fables fit only for old women. On the other hand, discipline yourself for the purpose of godliness;
texts.NKJV: But reject profane and old wives’ fables, and exercise yourself toward godliness.
texts.DRB: But avoid foolish and old wives' fables: and exercise thyself unto godliness.
texts.ESV: Have nothing to do with irreverent, silly myths. Rather train yourself for godliness;
texts.LEB: But reject those worthless myths told by elderly women, and train yourself for godliness.
texts.AMP: But have nothing to do with irreverent folklore and silly myths. On the other hand, discipline yourself for the purpose of godliness [keeping yourself spiritually fit].
texts.NLT: Do not waste time arguing over godless ideas and old wives’ tales. Instead, train yourself to be godly.
texts.AKJV: But refuse profane and old wives’ fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.
texts.NRSV: Have nothing to do with profane and foolish tales. Train yourself in godliness,

texts.NIV84: For physical training is of some value, but godliness has value for all things, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.
texts.KJV: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
texts.NASB95: for bodily discipline is only of little profit, but godliness is profitable for all things, since it holds promise for the present life and also for the life to come.
texts.NKJV: For bodily exercise profits a little, but godliness is profitable for all things, having promise of the life that now is and of that which is to come.
texts.DRB: For bodily exercise is profitable to little: but godliness is profitable to all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
texts.ESV: for while bodily training is of some value, godliness is of value in every way, as it holds promise for the present life and also for the life to come.
texts.LEB: For the training of the body is somewhat profitable, but godliness is profitable for everything, because it holds promise for the present life and for the life to come.
texts.AMP: For physical training is of some value, but godliness (spiritual training) is of value in everything and in every way, since it holds promise for the present life and for the life to come.
texts.NLT: “Physical training is good, but training for godliness is much better, promising benefits in this life and in the life to come.”
texts.AKJV: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
texts.NRSV: for, while physical training is of some value, godliness is valuable in every way, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.

texts.NIV84: This is a trustworthy saying that deserves full acceptance
texts.KJV: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.
texts.NASB95: It is a trustworthy statement deserving full acceptance.
texts.NKJV: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance.
texts.DRB: A faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.
texts.ESV: The saying is trustworthy and deserving of full acceptance.
texts.LEB: The statement is trustworthy and deserving of complete acceptance.
texts.AMP: This is a faithful and trustworthy saying worthy of full acceptance and approval.
texts.NLT: This is a trustworthy saying, and everyone should accept it.
texts.AKJV: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.
texts.NRSV: The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance.

texts.NIV84: (and for this we labor and strive), that we have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, and especially of those who believe.
texts.KJV: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.
texts.NASB95: For it is for this we labor and strive, because we have fixed our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of believers.
texts.NKJV: For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.
texts.DRB: For therefore we labor and are reviled, because we hope in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, especially of the faithful.
texts.ESV: For to this end we toil and strive, because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.
texts.LEB: For to this end we labor and suffer reproach, because we have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of believers.
texts.AMP: It is for this that we labor and strive [often called to account], because we have fixed our [confident] hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe [in Him, recognize Him as the Son of God, and accept Him as Savior and Lord].
texts.NLT: This is why we work hard and continue to struggle, for our hope is in the living God, who is the Savior of all people and particularly of all believers.
texts.AKJV: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.
texts.NRSV: For to this end we toil and suffer reproach, because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.

texts.NIV84: Command and teach these things.
texts.KJV: These things command and teach.
texts.NASB95: Prescribe and teach these things.
texts.NKJV: These things command and teach.
texts.DRB: These things command and teach.
texts.ESV: Command and teach these things.
texts.LEB: Command these things and teach them.
texts.AMP: Keep commanding and teaching these things.
texts.NLT: Teach these things and insist that everyone learn them.
texts.AKJV: These things command and teach.
texts.NRSV: Command and teach these things.

texts.NIV84: Don't let anyone look down on you because you are young, but set an example for the believers in speech, in life, in love, in faith and in purity.
texts.KJV: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
texts.NASB95: Let no one look down on your youthfulness, but rather in speech, conduct, love, faith and purity, show yourself an example of those who believe.
texts.NKJV: Let no one despise your youth, but be an example to the believers in word, in conduct, in love, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
texts.DRB: Let no man despise thy youth: but be thou an example of the faithful in word, in conversation, in charity, in faith, in chastity.
texts.ESV: Let no one despise you for your youth, but set the believers an example in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
texts.LEB: Let no one look down on your youth, but be an example for the believers in word, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
